---
title: "Day 3: Python and conda"
subtitle: "Morning brief, Wednesday July 15"
author: "Dan Negrut"
date: 2026-07-15
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, ../theme.scss]
    slide-number: true
    width: 1250
    margin: 0.08
    transition: fade
    footer: "ProCSI C Training  |  Day 3"
    embed-resources: true
---

## Where this fits

- Yesterday you learned to drive the computer by typing. Today you get the language you will use every remaining day: **Python**.
- The Euler loop, the Blender animation script, and PyChrono next week are all Python. This is the foundation day.
- **conda** is the sidekick: it installs Python and its packages for you, cleanly.

## Today's target

- **By 4:00, your team can:** run a small Python script of your own (variables, a loop, a function, a list) from the command line, inside a conda environment.
- **Bonus:** plot something with matplotlib, anything, even a straight line.

## How to start

- Install **Miniforge** (we do this together): it gives you `conda`, which manages Python for you.
- Create your camp environment once, then activate it every day:
  - `conda create -n camp python numpy matplotlib`
  - `conda activate camp`
- **No starter today.** Build your script from scratch, with Claude as your guide.
- Ask Claude: "Teach me Python variables, loops, and functions with tiny examples I can run" or "Give me five small Python exercises and check my answers."

## Optional side quest: git and GitHub {.smaller}

- Yesterday's `git clone` is all the git this camp needs. But if you are curious what you just used:
- **git** tracks versions of files on your machine; **GitHub** hosts shared copies in the cloud. Together they are how software teams work.
- Safe places to play and test your understanding:
  - **GitHub Skills** ([skills.github.com](https://skills.github.com)): official hands-on mini-courses in a practice repository (free account needed).
  - **Learn Git Branching** ([learngitbranching.js.org](https://learngitbranching.js.org)): a visual git sandbox in the browser, no account, nothing to install.
  - **first-contributions** ([github.com/firstcontributions/first-contributions](https://github.com/firstcontributions/first-contributions)): make your first real pull request in a beginner-friendly repository.

## Your end-of-day talk

- ~10 minutes: run your script live, and tell us one thing about Python that surprised you.
- **Start or Close with your team's quote of the day.**
- Stretch goal: the side quest above, or a function that does something fun (a countdown, a times table, a tiny guessing game).
